title: External AI applications
subtitle: Use supported commands, tools, and agents from other AI applications without copying their files into BitFun.
checkingNonBlocking: Still checking for changes. You can continue using settings while this finishes.

actions.updated: Saved. The current status is shown below.
actions.waitForUpdate: Another change is still being saved. Wait for it to finish before making another choice.
actions.agentUpdated: Saved changes to {{name}} and refreshed its status.

unavailable.title: External settings unavailable
unavailable.desktopOnly: External sources are currently available only in the desktop app.
unavailable.remoteWorkspace: Remote workspaces cannot use local external settings yet. BitFun did not load local settings in their place.

errors.loadFailed: BitFun could not load the current external settings. Do not assume external content is available; refresh to try again.
errors.refreshFailed: BitFun could not check for changes. Previously enabled content remains available, but recent changes may be missing; refresh to retry.
errors.mutationUnknown: BitFun could not confirm whether your change was saved. Do not assume an enable, disable, or source choice took effect; refresh to confirm the current state.

diagnostics.summary: {{count}} items need attention. Review the details to see what is affected.

category.confirmationStateUnavailable: BitFun could not verify saved tool confirmations, so affected tools remain disabled. Check BitFun settings storage, then refresh.
category.conflictHistoryUnavailable: BitFun could not save conflict information, so affected names remain unavailable. Check BitFun settings storage, then refresh.
category.checkInProgress: One source is still being checked. Existing content remains available.
category.checkTimedOut: Checking one source took too long. Other content remains available; refresh to try again.
category.confirmationRequired: A source needs your confirmation before BitFun can use it.
category.sourceTooLarge: Some files were skipped because the source is too large. Reduce its size, then refresh.
category.invalidSettings: Some settings could not be read and were skipped. Fix the source, then refresh.
category.unreadableSource: BitFun could not read part of a source. Check file access, then refresh.
category.notSupported: This type of external content is not supported yet, so BitFun did not load or run it.
category.checkFailed: BitFun could not check one source. Other sources remain available; refresh to retry.
category.sourceIssue: BitFun found an issue in one source. The affected content was not enabled; review the technical details.

sources.title: Configuration sources
sources.description: Settings stay in their original locations. Open this page to check for changes automatically.
sources.globalOnly: Showing sources available to all workspaces. Open a workspace to also show sources from that project.
sources.empty: No supported external settings were found.

agents.title: Found agents
agents.description: Agents stay in their original application. Before enabling one, review its model, tools, and where it runs. BitFun asks again if its instructions or these settings change.
agents.approvalWarning: Once enabled, this agent can instruct the selected model to use the tools shown above. BitFun asks again if its instructions, model, tools, or configuration sources change.
agents.executionDomain: Runs in: this computer and current workspace

agentChanges.unavailable: {{name}} is no longer available for new tasks ({{state}}). Review its current model, tools, source, or name conflict below.
agentChanges.unavailableMany: {{count}} enabled agents are no longer available for new tasks. Review their current settings below.

agentCompatibility.ready_with_degradation: Supported, but some settings will not apply

agentDiagnostics.activationBlocked: This agent cannot be enabled yet.
agentDiagnostics.degradedOnly: Some settings will not apply in BitFun. Review the details to confirm the resulting behavior is acceptable.

configurationUnavailable.reason: BitFun could not read its model settings.
configurationUnavailable.nextStep: Open BitFun model settings and confirm they load. If not, restart BitFun. If the problem continues, check that BitFun can read and save its settings; then refresh.

modelUnavailable.reason: The requested model is not available in BitFun.
modelUnavailable.nextStep: Choose an available model in the source application, or set an available fixed Sub-Agent model in BitFun, then refresh.

toolUnavailable.reason: One or more requested tools are not available in BitFun.
toolUnavailable.nextStep: Remove or replace the unsupported tools in the source application, then refresh.

invalidDefinition.reason: The agent settings have an invalid or missing required value.
invalidDefinition.nextStep: Correct the agent settings in the source application, then refresh.

unsupportedBehavior.reason: This agent requires behavior or settings that BitFun does not support.
unsupportedBehavior.nextStep: Update the agent in the source application to use supported settings and include all required content, then refresh.

ignoredOption.reason: BitFun does not apply this setting.
ignoredOption.nextStep: Before enabling, review the model and tools that will actually be used, and confirm that the result still meets your expectations.

agentConflicts.title: Choose between agents with the same name
agentConflicts.description: BitFun will not choose one for you. Your choice is remembered until one of these agents changes.
agentConflicts.disableAll: Do not use any of these
agentConflicts.pending: Choose which agent to use for future tasks. Choosing an external agent also confirms the model, tools, and where it runs as shown below.
agentConflicts.selectionApproves: This choice confirms the model, tools, run location, and configuration sources shown below.

tools.title: Found tools
tools.description: Tool code stays in its original location. BitFun does not run it while checking the source.
tools.targetScope: This action applies to every tool provided by this source.

toolApprovals.title: Tools to review
toolApprovals.description: Before enabling tool code from another application, review where the code comes from and what it can access.
toolApprovals.warning: Tool code runs with your user permissions and inherits your environment variables. It is not isolated by an operating-system sandbox. Processes it starts may keep running after you cancel.

toolReason.approval_required: Review this tool file's access before enabling it.
toolReason.disabled: You chose to disable this tool source.
toolReason.active: The tool code is loaded and ready to use.
toolReason.conflict: Another tool has the same name.
toolReason.unsupported: This tool file contains code or operations that BitFun does not support.
toolReason.runtime_unavailable: The required JavaScript run environment is unavailable.
toolReason.load_failed: BitFun could not load this tool file.

toolNextStep.approval_required: Review the source and access above, then enable or keep these tools disabled.
toolNextStep.disabled: Review the source and access above before enabling these tools.
toolNextStep.active: No action needed. Disable these tools to stop using this source's tools.
toolNextStep.conflict: Choose which tool to use below, or leave them disabled.
toolNextStep.unsupported: Change the source to a single JavaScript file supported by BitFun, then refresh.
toolNextStep.runtime_unavailable: Install or repair Node.js, then restart BitFun to check again.
toolNextStep.load_failed: Fix the source and refresh, or keep these tools disabled.

toolConflicts.title: Choose between tools with the same name
toolConflicts.description: Until you choose, BitFun keeps using an available local or MCP tool. If none is available, this name stays disabled. You will not be asked again unless the available choices change.
toolConflicts.pending: Choose which tool to use when this name is called.

conflicts.title: Choose between commands with the same name
conflicts.description: When multiple sources provide the same command, BitFun will not choose one for you. Your choice is remembered until one of the sources changes.
conflicts.pending: Choose an available source to use this command.
